# flashblade_lifecycle_rule (Data Source)

Reads an existing FlashBlade bucket lifecycle rule by bucket name and rule ID.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "flashblade_lifecycle_rule" "example" {
  bucket_name = "my-bucket"
  rule_id     = "rule-001"
}

output "lifecycle_rule_enabled" {
  value = data.flashblade_lifecycle_rule.example.enabled
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `bucket_name` (String) The name of the bucket.
- `rule_id` (String) The rule identifier within the bucket.

### Read-Only

- `abort_incomplete_multipart_uploads_after` (Number) Duration in milliseconds after which incomplete multipart uploads are aborted.
- `cleanup_expired_object_delete_marker` (Boolean) Whether expired object delete markers are cleaned up.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the lifecycle rule is enabled.
- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the lifecycle rule.
- `keep_current_version_for` (Number) Duration in milliseconds to keep current object versions before expiration.
- `keep_current_version_until` (Number) Timestamp in milliseconds until which current object versions are kept.
- `keep_previous_version_for` (Number) Duration in milliseconds to keep previous object versions before expiration.
- `prefix` (String) Object key prefix filter for the rule.
